$comment: TF-C27 shipped <stdio.h> `fileno` witness (D-SHIPPED-SYMBOL-PER-TARGET-AVAILABILITY, the unistd.json fdatasync/fallocate precedent). stdio.json now ships `int fileno(FILE*)` gated availableObjectFormats:[elf] because a real consumer landed (sqlite src/test_quota.c calls fsync/ftruncate/fstat(fileno(p->f)) on the POSIX path). fileno is referenced (links + loads dynamic libc.so.6 — the eager-import proof: glibc exports it WEAK `W fileno@@GLIBC_2.2.5` x86_64 + `W fileno@@GLIBC_2.17` arm64, present=resolvable at load) and its runtime RETURN VALUES are the witness: POSIX maps the three standard streams to fds 0/1/2, so fileno(stdin/stdout/stderr) == 0/1/2 -> exit 42 on a real Linux runtime; a broken binding never yields 42. Two elf targets only (fileno gated [elf]; pe would need _fileno + a fileno->_fileno macro, macho has no consumer yet -- both deferred, fail loud). Runs on linux-gcc + native arm64 CI legs; cross-compiles + qemu-runs the arm64 target locally. Release arm runs the optimizer over the three opaque external calls. RED-ON-DISABLE: drop fileno from stdio.json -> S0001 -> compile fails.
